title: rumex tuberosus subsp. creticus
intro: Rumex tuberosus subsp. creticus, also known as Cretan dock, is a perennial plant in the buckwheat family that grows in the Mediterranean region and has edible tubers and leaves. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it effective in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rumex tuberosus subsp. creticus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and protecting against oxidative stress, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or decoction to treat various health issues, and the tubers can be cooked and consumed as a vegetable.
